mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It provides automated scaling, monitoring, and updates, making it ideal for production workloads. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's infrastructure and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This step installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and runs the installation script.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud. Specify the desired version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --name my-cluster --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..example --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..example --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace 'example' with your actual OCIDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --name my-cluster --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..example --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..example --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..example --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --overwrite

```

> Ensure to replace 'example' with your actual cluster OCID.
commands: oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..example --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --overwrite

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster, as well as RBAC for security.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/rbac.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the RBAC configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..example --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> Replace 'example' with your actual cluster OCID.
commands: oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..example --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id ocid1.loadbalancer.oc1..example --force

```

> Replace 'example' with your actual load balancer OCID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..example --kubernetes-version 1.30.2

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'example' with your actual cluster OCID.
> Verify that the nodes are updated and in 'Ready' status.

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Verification
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that all components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check that all pods are running without issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running OCI CLI commands.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your OCI CLI is not properly authenticated.

**Fix:**
Run 'oci setup config' to reconfigure your OCI CLI credentials.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the node is not able to communicate with the control plane.

**Fix:**
Check the node's network configuration and ensure it has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the required subnets are not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Verify that the load balancer subnets are correctly set up in your VCN.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are stuck in 'Pending' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Scale up your node pool or check resource requests/limits for your pods.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new OCI features such as improved load balancer configurations and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated APIs related to older Kubernetes versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure to update your configurations to align with the new API specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: oci ce cluster create --name my-cluster --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..example --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..example --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..example --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --overwrite

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
